data.title: Inhibit Progression of Coronary Artery Calcification With Vitamin K in HemoDialysis Patients: The iPACK-HD Study
data.brief_summary: The purpose of this study is to see if vitamin K supplementation three times per week reduces the progression of coronary artery calcification over 12 months in dialysis patients compared to placebo.
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Able to provide signed informed consent
* ≥18 years of age
* Expected to survive one year
* Have end-stage kidney disease and require hemodialysis
* Have a baseline coronary artery calcification score ≥30 Agatston units (AUs)

data.sponsor: Clinical Evaluation Research Unit at Kingston General Hospital
